# Redis Connector - Full Documentation

# Redis Connector 5.6

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Connector for Remote Dictionary Server (Redis Connector) is an open source, in-memory data
structure store used to cache user profiles, create leader boards, track real-time activities on a
website, and filter spam. You can use Redis Connector as a message queue, custom object store, and
more.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Redis API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements in a Mule flow
- Global elements
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have access to the Redis target resource.

## Connection Types

> [!NOTE] Redis Connector supports only password-based authentication. Redis Connector does not
> currently support Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

Redis Connector uses the following connection types:

- Clustered
- NonClustered
- Sentinel

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites and experiment with examples, you are ready to create an app
and configure the connector using [Anypoint Studio](redis-connector-studio).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Redis in Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-redis-connector/)
- [Redis in EU Anypoint Exchange](https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/com.mulesoft.connectors/mule-redis-connector/)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Redis Connector 5.6 Additional Configuration Information

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Redis Connector contributes an object store implementation. Although Redis offers flexible eviction
and persistence, you must understand how the Mule Redis-backed object store is designed to work when
Redis is shared across multiple applications or systems. Learn key design constraints, eviction
behavior, and best practices for using Redis as an object store in Mule applications, with a focus
on avoiding deserialization and eviction-related failures.

## Redis as a Mule Object Store

When Redis is configured as an object store in Mule:

- Mule serializes payloads before storing them in Redis.
- Object Store operations (retrieve, remove, expire) require successful deserialization of stored
  values.

As a result, the Redis object store is designed to manage only entries that it created and can
deserialize.

## Common Deployment Patterns

In some architectures, Redis is deployed as a shared service and accessed by:

- Multiple Mule applications
- External (non-Mule) applications
- Operational or administrative scripts that insert or delete keys

This pattern is valid for Redis itself but introduces important considerations when Redis is used
specifically as a Mule object store.

## Examples of Object Store Behavior

Here are some scenarios of how the Redis object store behaves when Redis is shared across multiple
applications or systems.

### In-Memory Object Store per Application

![App A and App B, each connecting to its own cloud service and dedicated database to store unique access and refresh tokens](_images/redis-scenario-1.png)

Setup

- Application A and Application B each use an in-memory object store.

Outcome

- Each application maintains its own isolated store.
- No key collisions or deserialization issues occur.
- Expiration and invalidation are fully controlled per application.

This is the safest and most isolated configuration.

### Shared External Object Store (Not Redis)

![App A and App B sharing a single database that stores multiple sets of access and refresh tokens](_images/redis-scenario-2.png)

Setup

- Application A and Application B use a common external object store.
- Identical object IDs (for example, access_token) are used.

Outcome

- Redis object store implementation prevents overwriting entries created by another application.
- This behavior is intentional to prevent data corruption.

Cross-application data ownership is restricted by design.

### Redis as a Shared External Object Store

![App A and App B both connecting to a centralized Redis database while also maintaining individual connections to Salesforce and ServiceNow](_images/redis-scenario-3.png)

Setup

- Redis is configured as a shared external object store.
- Mule Application A, Application B, and external systems write to the same Redis instance.
- Stored data includes:
  - Java-serialized objects (via Mule object store)
  - Plain text or JSON (via direct Redis operations)

Outcome

- During eviction or expiration, the Mule object store might encounter entries it canât deserialize.
- This can result in errors such as:

  ```text
  org.mule.runtime.api.store.ObjectStoreException: Could not deserialize object
  Caused by: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException
  ```

Eviction or cleanup fails when Redis contains entries not owned by the current application or
classloader.

## Expectation vs. Actual Behavior

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Expectation</th><th>Actual Behavior</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Redis object store can be freely shared across applications.</p></td><td><p>Each application can only deserialize objects created within its own classloader.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration task cleans all excess entries.</p></td><td><p>Expiration fails when encountering unknown or incompatible entries.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Non-Mule data can coexist in Redis.</p></td><td><p>Non-serialized data can cause object store failures.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cache Scope can be invalidated across applications.</p></td><td><p>Cache invalidation is application-bound.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Why This Behavior Is by Design

The Redis object store follows strict safety and isolation principles:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Design Principle</th><th>Explanation</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Data Ownership</p></td><td><p>Manages only data that the object store owns</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Data Integrity</p></td><td><p>Prevents accidental deletion of external or foreign data</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Classloader Isolation</p></td><td><p>Deserializes objects only from its own Mule application</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fail-Safe Operation</p></td><td><p>Fails safely instead of performing destructive deletes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

This ensures predictable behavior and avoids unintended side effects in shared environments.

## Redis Eviction and Mule Object Store

Redis eviction policies operate at the Redis level, not at the Mule object store level. Mule object
store expiration deserializes values even during expiration. As a result, Mule object store
expiration can fail if incompatible entries exist.

## Recommended Best Practices

- Separate Object Store and Generic Redis Usage
  - Use object store APIs only for Mule-managed, serialized data stored in Redis object store.
  - Use Redis Connector operations for plain text, JSON, or external data.

- Namespace Keys Explicitly
  - For shared Redis object store, ensure object store keys donât overlap with externally managed
    Redis keys.
---

# Redis Connector 5.6 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

The following examples show how to use Redis Connector to set a key value on the Redis server and
how to use Redis Connector as a custom object store in a Mule app.

## Set a Key Value on the Redis Server

The following screenshot shows the flow for this example:

![Studio flow with Listener, Set, and Set Payload](_images/redis-set-raw-flow.png)

To use Anypoint Studio to set a key value on the Redis server:

1.  Create a new Mule project by clicking **File** > **New** > **Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for the new project and click **Finish**.
3.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **HTTP** > **Listener** and drag it to the Studio canvas.

    HTTP Listener is the entry point for the flow and provides the key and its value.

4.  Click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector Configuration** field.
5.  Leave the default configuration and click **OK**.
6.  Set the **Path** field to `/`.
7.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `redis` and drag the **Set** operation to the right of
    **Listener**.

    The **Set** operation sends data to the Redis server.

8.  Set the properties for the **Set** operation as follows:
    - Set the **Display Name** field to `Set value for key into Redis`.
    - Set the **Key** field to `#[payload.key]`.
    - Set the **Value** field to `#[payload.value]`:

      ![Set operation properties window on General tab](_images/redis-set-config.png)

9.  In the **Mule Palette** view, search for `payload` and drag the **Set Payload** component to the
    right of **Set value for key into Redis** on the Studio canvas.

    The **Set Payload** component creates the response for the incoming HTTP request.

10. Set the **Set Payload** properties as follows:
    - Set the **Display Name** field to `Set value response`.
    - Set the **Value** field to `Successfully set value: #[payload.value] to key: #[payload.key]`:

      ![Set Payload properties window on General tab](_images/redis-set-response-config.png)

11. Save your changes and deploy the app by right-clicking the Studio canvas and selecting **Run
    project <project name>**.
12. When the app is running, use an HTTP client app to send a POST request to `localhost:8081/` with
    content-type `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` and a body in `url-encoded` format.

    The request body should contain a key and value:

    `curl -X POST -d "key=test-key" -d "value=test-value" localhost:8081/`

The XML for this example should look like this:

```text
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:redis="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis"
        xmlns:tracking="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking"
        xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
        xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
        xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
        xmlns:spring="http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans"
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans
        http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans/spring-beans-current.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking/current/mule-tracking-ee.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis/current/mule-redis.xsd">
    <redis:config
            name="Redis__Configuration"
            host="${config.host}"
            connectionTimeout="${config.connectionTimeout}"
            port="${config.port}"
            doc:name="Redis: Configuration"/>
â
    <http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" basePath="/">
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8081" />
	</http:listener-config>
	<flow name="set-flow">
        <http:listener
                config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
                path="/"
                doc:name="Set value HTTP endpoint"/>
        <redis:set
                config-ref="Redis__Configuration"
                key="#[payload.key]"
                value="#[payload.value]"
        doc:name="Set value for key into Redis">
			<redis:value ><![CDATA[payload.key]]></redis:value>
		</redis:set>
        <set-payload
         value="Successfully set value: #[payload.value] to key: #[payload.key]"
         doc:name="Set value response"/>
    </flow>
</mule>
```

## Use Redis Connector as a Custom Object Store

In Studio to configure Redis Connector as a custom object store in a Mule app:

1.  Add the Redis namespace to the `<mule>` element:

    ```none
    xmlns:redis="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis"
    ```

2.  Add the location of the Redis schema referred to by the Redis namespace:

    ```text
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis/current/mule-redis.xsd
    ```

3.  Add the HTTP namespace to the `<mule>` element:

    ```none
    xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
    ```

4.  Add the location of the HTTP schema referred to by the HTTP namespace:

    ```text
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
    ```

5.  Add the object store namespace to the `<mule>` element:

    ```none
    xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
    ```

6.  Add the location of the object store schema referred to by the object storeâs namespace:

    ```text
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
    ```

7.  Using the Studio XML editor, add an `<os:config>` element to your project and configure it to
    contain a Redis connection type (`clustered-connection` or `nonclustered-connection`).

    ```xml
    <os:config name="ObjectStore_Config">
         <redis:nonclustered-connection host="${redis.host}"/>
    </os:config>
    ```

8.  Add an `<os:object-store>` element to your project and configure it to reference the
    configuration created previously:

    ```xml
    <os:object-store
    	name="Object_store"
    	config-ref="ObjectStore_Config"
    	maxEntries="1"
    	entryTtl="60"
    	expirationInterval="10"
    	expirationIntervalUnit="SECONDS"/>
    ```

9.  Add an `<http:listener-config>` element to your project and configure its attributes:

    ```xml
    <http:listener-config
    	name="HTTP_Listener_Configuration"
    	host="0.0.0.0"
     	port="8081" doc:name="Listener"/>
    ```

10. Add an empty `<flow>` element to your project:

    ```xml
    <<flow> name="set-flow">
    </flow>
    ```

11. Within the `<flow>` element, add an `<http:listener>` element:

    ```xml
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Configuration" path="/" />
    ```

12. Within the `<flow>` element, add an `<os:store>` element after the `<http:listener>` element and
    configure `<os:store>` to use the object store created previously:

    ```xml
    <os:store
    	key="#[attributes.queryParams.key]"
    	objectStore="Object_store"
    	failIfPresent="true"
    	failOnNullValue="false">
    	<os:value ><![CDATA[#[attributes.queryParams.value]]]></os:value>
    </os:store>
    ```

    When youâre done, the XML file should look like this:

    ```xml
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    â
    <mule xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
            xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
            xmlns:os="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os"
            xmlns:redis="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis"
            xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
            xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
            xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
            xsi:schemaLocation="
    http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis/current/mule-redis.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/os/current/mule-os.xsd
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
            http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd">
    â
            <http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config">
                    <http:listener-connection
                    host="0.0.0.0"
                    port="8081" />
            </http:listener-config>
    â
            <os:object-store
                    name="Object_store"
                    config-ref="ObjectStore_Config"
                    maxEntries="1"
                    entryTtl="60"
                    expirationInterval="10"
                    expirationIntervalUnit="SECONDS"/>
    â
            <os:config name="ObjectStore_Config">
            <redis:nonclustered-connection
                            host="${redis.host}"/>
                </os:config>
    â
            <flow name="StoreFlow" >
            <http:listener
                    doc:name="Listener"
                    config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config"
                            path="/store"/>
    		<os:store
                            doc:name="Store"
                            key="#[attributes.queryParams.key]"
                            objectStore="Object_store"
                            failIfPresent="true"
                            failOnNullValue="false">
                    <os:value ><![CDATA[#[attributes.queryParams.value]]]></os:value>
            </os:store>
                </flow>
    </mule>
    ```

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Redis Connector 5.6 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

> [!NOTE] You can set configurations in Expression mode, except when Redis Connector is used in
> conjunction with Object Store Connector, which does not support Expression mode.

Starting with Redis Connector 5.4.5, Mule apps that use an Object Store **Entry TTL** exceeding 24
days now observe that the TTL for new keys is set to the correct values.

## Redis Configuration

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#redis_clustered">Clustered</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#redis_nonclustered">NonClustered</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#redis_sentinel">Sentinel</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the instance expires at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connection Types

> [!NOTE] Redis Connector supports only password-based authentication. Redis Connector does not
> currently support Role-Based Access Control (RBAC).

### Clustered

> [!NOTE] Redis Connector does not support clustered connections on Redis Enterprise Active-Active
> databases. To connect to one of the Redis Enterprise active-active clusters, non-clustered
> connection should be used in the connector. However, the connection is only made to the specified
> node.
>
> Redis Connector does not support clustered sharded connections.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint identification algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTPS</p></li><li><p>LDAPS</p></li><li><p>DISABLED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The endpoint identification algorithm used by clients to validate the server hostname. The default value is an empty string, which means that this functionality is disabled. Clients, including client connections created by the broker for inter-broker communication, verify that the broker hostname matches the hostname in the brokerâs certificate.</p></td><td><p>DISABLED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If you are running the Mule app securely by using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS in the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Total</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections that can be in the pool at a given time</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of idle connections allowed in a pool before the connections are closed</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum number of idle connections in the pool that are ready for immediate use</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lifo</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the queue is last-in first-out</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fairness</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the thread that waited for the longest amount of time to connect acquires the next available connection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Wait Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of milliseconds the client can wait before a connection is available</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Evictable Idle Time Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time a connection can be idle in the pool before it is evicted</p></td><td><p>1800000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Min Evictable Idle Time Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time a connection can be idle in pool before it is evicted</p></td><td><p>1800000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Num Tests Per Eviction Run</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of connections to test per eviction attempt</p></td><td><p>3</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test On Create</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test On Borrow</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection before it is borrowed from the pool</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test On Return</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection before it is returned from the pool</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test While Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to enable idle resource detection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Between Eviction Runs Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Cycle of time for idle resources detection</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Block When Exhausted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the client waits when the resource pool is exhausted</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmx Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables JMX monitoring when set to <code>true</code></p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmx Name Prefix</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMX name prefix used as part of the name assigned to the JMX-enabled pool</p></td><td><p>pool</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmx Name Base</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMX name base used as part of the name assigned to the JMX-enabled pool</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis instance Connection timeout value.</p></td><td><p>2000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for Redis instance connection timeout.</p></td><td><p>MILLISECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis password. Set if the server requires authentication.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry TTL</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The default time to live to set on keys stored for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cluster Hosts</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis cluster hosts separated by commas and having the format <code>host:port</code>. Use this field when you want to access your Redis cluster through the connector.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### NonClustered

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint identification algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTPS</p></li><li><p>LDAPS</p></li><li><p>DISABLED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The endpoint identification algorithm used by clients to validate the server hostname. The default value is an empty string, which means that this functionality is disabled. Clients, including client connections created by the broker for inter-broker communication, verify that the broker hostname matches the hostname in the brokerâs certificate.</p></td><td><p>DISABLED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If you are running the Mule app securely by using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS in the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Total</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections that can be in the pool at a given time</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of idle connections allowed in a pool before the connections are closed</p></td><td><p>8</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum number of idle connections in the pool that are ready for immediate use</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Lifo</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the queue is last-in first-out</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fairness</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the thread that waited for the longest amount of time to connect acquires the next available connection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Wait Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of milliseconds the client can wait before a connection is available</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min Evictable Idle Time Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time a connection can be idle in the pool before it is evicted</p></td><td><p>1800000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Min Evictable Idle Time Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time a connection can be idle in pool before it is evicted</p></td><td><p>1800000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Num Tests Per Eviction Run</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of connections to test per eviction attempt</p></td><td><p>3</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test On Create</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test On Borrow</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection before it is borrowed from the pool</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test On Return</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection before it is returned from the pool</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Test While Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to enable idle resource detection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Between Eviction Runs Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Cycle of time for idle resources detection</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Block When Exhausted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the client waits when the resource pool is exhausted</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmx Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables JMX monitoring when set to <code>true</code></p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmx Name Prefix</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMX name prefix used as part of the name assigned to the JMX-enabled pool</p></td><td><p>pool</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Jmx Name Base</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMX name base used as part of the name assigned to the JMX-enabled pool</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis instance Connection timeout value.</p></td><td><p>2000</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for Redis instance connection timeout.</p></td><td><p>MILLISECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis password. Set this if the server requires authentication.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Entry TTL</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The default time to live to be set on keys stored for the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Host</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis host. Use this field when you want to access you Redis server that is not part of a cluster through connector.</p></td><td><p>localhost</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Port</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis port. The port on which your non cluster server is running.</p></td><td><p>6379</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Sentinel

Sentinel provides a high-availability solution in case of failure on your Redis cluster.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Endpoint identification algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTPS</p></li><li><p>LDAPS</p></li><li><p>DISABLED</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The endpoint identification algorithm used by clients to validate the server hostname. The default value is an empty string, which means that this functionality is disabled. Clients, including client connections created by the broker for inter-broker communication, verify that the broker hostname matches the hostname in the brokerâs certificate.</p></td><td><p>DISABLED</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If you are running the Mule app securely by using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS in the connector.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Max Total</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of connections that can be in the pool at a given time</p></td><td><p>8</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Max Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of idle connections allowed in a pool before the connections are closed</p></td><td><p>8</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Min Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum number of idle connections in the pool that are ready for immediate use</p></td><td><p>0</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Lifo</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the queue is last-in first-out</p></td><td><p>true</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Fairness</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If set to <code>true</code>, the thread that waited for the longest amount of time to connect acquires the next available connection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Max Wait Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of milliseconds the client can wait before a connection is available</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Min Evictable Idle Time Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time a connection can be idle in the pool before it is evicted</p></td><td><p>1800000</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Soft Min Evictable Idle Time Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Minimum amount of time a connection can be idle in pool before it is evicted</p></td><td><p>1800000</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Num Tests Per Eviction Run</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of connections to test per eviction attempt</p></td><td><p>3</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Test On Create</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Test On Borrow</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection before it is borrowed from the pool</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Test On Return</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to test the validity of the connection before it is returned from the pool</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Test While Idle</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to enable idle resource detection</p></td><td><p>false</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Time Between Eviction Runs Millis</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Cycle of time for idle resources detection</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Block When Exhausted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the client waits when the resource pool is exhausted</p></td><td><p>true</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Jmx Enabled</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Enables JMX monitoring when set to <code>true</code></p></td><td><p>true</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Jmx Name Prefix</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMX name prefix used as part of the name assigned to the JMX-enabled pool</p></td><td><p>pool</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Jmx Name Base</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>JMX name base used as part of the name assigned to the JMX-enabled pool</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis instance connection timeout value.</p></td><td><p>2000</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Connection Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for Redis instance connection timeout.</p></td><td><p>MILLISECONDS</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis password. Set this if the server requires authentication.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Entry TTL</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The default time to live to be set on keys stored for the connector.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Sentinels Hosts</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of sentinel addresses that are monitoring the master host to which the connector will connect.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td><td><p>Master Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Redis master name. The name of the master for which the sentinels will return the current address.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Sentinel Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sentinel password. Set this if the sentinel servers require authentication.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Index</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The index of the database that the pool will use.</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Sentinel Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Sentinel connection timeout value.</p></td><td><p>2000</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Sentinel Connection Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for sentinel connection timeout.</p></td><td><p>MILLISECONDS</p></td></tr><tr><td></td><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

Supported source:

- [Subscribe](#subscribe)

### Subscribe

`<redis:subscribe>`

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channels</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A list of channel names or globbing patterns.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this source outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should be executed on the primary node only when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#repeatable-in-memory-stream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#repeatable-file-store-stream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#non-repeatable-stream">Non-repeatable Stream</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#SubscribeChannelAttributes">Subscribe Channel Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

## Operations

Supported operations:

- [Add To Set](#addToSet)
- [Add To Sorted Set](#addToSortedSet)
- [Decrement](#decrement)
- [Del](#del)
- [Exists](#exists)
- [Expire](#expire)
- [Expire At](#expireAt)
- [Get](#get)
- [Get All From Hash](#getAllFromHash)
- [Get From Hash](#getFromHash)
- [Get Range By Index](#getRangeByIndex)
- [Get Range By Score](#getRangeByScore)
- [Get TTL](#getTtl)
- [Increment](#increment)
- [Increment Hash](#incrementHash)
- [Increment Sorted Set](#incrementSortedSet)
- [Persist](#persist)
- [Pop From List](#popFromList)
- [Pop From Set](#popFromSet)
- [Publish](#publish)
- [Push To List](#pushToList)
- [Random Member From Set](#randomMemberFromSet)
- [Range From List](#rangeFromList)
- [Set](#set)
- [Set In Hash](#setInHash)
- [Trim From List](#trimFromList)

### Add To Set

`<redis:add-to-set>`

Add the message payload to the set stored at the specified key. If the key does not exist, a new key
holding a set is created.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for SADD</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value to set.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Must Succeed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, ensures that adding to the set is successful (that is, no pre-existing identical value is in the set)</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Add To Sorted Set

`<redis:add-to-sorted-set>`

Add the message payload with the desired score to the sorted set stored at the specified key. If the
key does not exist, a new key holding a sorted set is created.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for ZADD</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value to set.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Score</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Score to use for the value</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Must Succeed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, ensures that adding to the set is successful (that is, no pre-existing identical value is in the set)</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Decrement

`<redis:decrement>`

Decrements the number stored at key by step. If the key does not exist, it is set to 0 before
performing the operation. An error is returned if the key contains a value of the wrong type or
contains data that cannot be represented as integer.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for DECR.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Step</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Step used for the increment.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Del

`<redis:del>`

Remove the specified key, if it exists.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for DEL</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Exists

`<redis:exists>`

Test if the specified key exists.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for EXISTS</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Expire

`<redis:expire>`

Set a timeout on the specified key.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key in the sorted set.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Seconds</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The time to live in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Expire At

`<redis:expire-at>`

Set a timeout in the form of a UNIX timestamp (number of seconds elapsed since 1 Jan 1970) on the
specified key.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key in the sorted set.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Unix Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The UNIX timestamp in seconds.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Get

`<redis:get>`

Get the value of the specified key. If the key does not exist, a null value is returned.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for GET</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Get All From Hash

`<redis:get-all-from-hash>`

Get all fields and values of the hash stored at the specified key. If the field or the hash donât
exist, a null value is returned.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for HGETALL</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Object</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Get From Hash

`<redis:get-from-hash>`

Get the value stored at the specified field in the hash at the specified key. If the field or the
hash donât exist, a null value is returned.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for HGET</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Field</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Field to use for HGET</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
