- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Get Range By Index

`<redis:get-range-by-index>`

Retrieve a range of values from the sorted set stored at the specified key. The range of values is
defined by indices in the sorted set and sorted as desired.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for ZRANGE/ZREVRANGE</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Range start index</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>End</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Range end index</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ascending Order</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Index order for sorting the range. If the parameter is set to <code>true</code>, the range is sorted in ascending order. If the parameter is set to <code>false</code>, the range is sorted in descending order.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Get Range By Score

`<redis:get-range-by-score>`

Retrieve a range of values from the sorted set stored at the specified key. The range of values is
defined by scores in the sorted set and sorted as desired.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for ZRANGEBYSCORE and ZREVRANGEBYSCORE</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Min</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Range start score</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Range end score</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ascending Order</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Score order for sorting the range. If the parameter is set to <code>true</code>, the range is sorted in ascending order. If the parameter is set to <code>false</code>, the range is sorted in descending order.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Get TTL

`<redis:get-ttl>`

Get the remaining time to live of a volatile key, in seconds.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key in the sorted set.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Increment

`<redis:increment>`

Increments the number stored at key by step. If the key does not exist, it is set to 0 before
performing the operation. An error is returned if the key contains a value of the wrong type or
contains data that can not be represented as integer.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for INCR.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Step</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Step used for the increment.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Increment Hash

`<redis:increment-hash>`

Increments the number stored at field in the hash stored at key by increment. If the key does not
exist, a new key holding a hash is created. If field does not exist, the value is set to `0` before
the operation is performed.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for HGET</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Field</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Field to use for HGET</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Step</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Step used for the increment.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Increment Sorted Set

`<redis:increment-sorted-set>`

Increments the score of member in the sorted set stored at key by increment. If member does not
exist in the sorted set, it is added with increment as its score (as if its previous score was
`0.0`). If the key does not exist, a new sorted set with the specified member as its sole member is
created.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key in the sorted set.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value to set.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Step</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The step to use to increment the score.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Number</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Persist

`<redis:persist>`

Undo an expire or expireAt, turning the volatile key into a normal key.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The key in the sorted set.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Pop From List

`<redis:pop-from-list>`

Pop a value from the desired side of the list stored at the specified key.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for LPOP/RPOP</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pop Left</p></td><td><div><div><p>boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>The side from which to pop the value. If the parameter is <code>true</code>, the value is popped from the left side. If the parameter is <code>false</code>, the value is popped from the right side.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Pop From Set

`<redis:pop-from-set>`

Pops a random value from the set stored at the specified key.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for SPOP</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Publish

`<redis:publish>`

Publish the message payload to the specified channel.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Channel</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Destination of the published message</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The message to publish.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Boolean</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Push To List

`<redis:push-to-list>`

Push the message payload to the desired side (LEFT or RIGHT) of the list stored at the specified
key. If the key does not exist, a new key holding a list is created as long as ifExists is `false`.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for LPUSH or RPUSH, or LPUSHX or RPUSHX.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value to push.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Push Left</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Side that receives the pushed payload. If the parameter is true, push the payload on the left side. If the parameter is <code>false</code>, push the payload on the right side.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Exists</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If the parameter is <code>true</code>, execute LPUSHX or RPUSHX. If the parameter is <code>false</code>, execute LPUSH or RPUSH.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Random Member From Set

`<redis:random-member-from-set>`

Reads a random value from the set stored at the specified key.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for SRANDMEMBER</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Range From List

`<redis:range-from-list>`

Returns the specified range of elements from an existing list.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Key of the list from which to return elements</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>start</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Zero-based index at which the range starts. A negative value indicates that the offset starts at the end of the list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>stop</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Zero-based index at which the range ends. A negative value indicates that the offset starts at the end of the list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Set

`<redis:set>`

Set the key to hold the payload. If the key already holds a value, it is overwritten, regardless of
its type as long as ifNotExists is `false`.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key used to store payload</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value to set.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expire</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set a timeout on the specified key in seconds. After the timeout the key is automatically deleted by the server. A key with an associated timeout is said to be volatile in Redis terminology.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Not Exists</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, then execute SETNX on the Redis server, otherwise execute SET.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The expression outcome is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Set In Hash

`<redis:set-in-hash>`

Set the specified hash field to the message payload. If the key does not exist, a new key holding a
hash is created as long as ifNotExists is true.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Key to use for HSET</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The value to set.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Field</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Field to use for HSET</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>If Not Exists</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code> execute HSETNX, otherwise HSET.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

### Trim From List

`<redis:trim-from-list>`

Modify an existing list so that it contains only the specified range of elements.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Key of the list to modify</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>start</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Zero-based index at which the range starts. A negative value indicates that the offset starts at the end of the list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>stop</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Zero-based index at which the range ends. A negative value indicates that the offset starts at the end of the list.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of a variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates against the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the target variable.</p></td><td><p><code>#[payload]</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Redis Configuration](#redis)

#### Throws

- REDIS:CONNECTIVITY
- REDIS:INVALID_REQUEST_DATA
- REDIS:INVALID_CONFIGURATION
- REDIS:INVALID_STRUCTURE_FOR_INPUT_DATA
- REDIS:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- REDIS:UNKNOWN
- REDIS:UNABLE_TO_UNSUBSCRIBE

## Types

### TLS

Configures TLS to provide secure communications for the Mule app.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Enabled Protocols</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enabled Cipher Suites</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Trust Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Truststore</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the TLS truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Store</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Keystore</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the TLS keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revocation Check</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#standard-revocation-check">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#custom-ocsp-responder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#crl-file">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Truststore

Configures the truststore for TLS.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the truststore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to protect the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Type of store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encryption algorithm that the truststore uses.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Insecure</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule stops performing certificate validations. Setting this to <code>true</code> can make connections vulnerable to attacks.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Keystore

Configures the keystore for the TLS protocol. The keystore you generate contains a private key and a
public certificate.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the keystore. Mule resolves the path relative to the current classpath and file system.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Type of store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the key to use when the keystore contains multiple private keys. By default, Mule uses the first key in the file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Key Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to protect the private key.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Password used to protect the keystore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encryption algorithm that the keystore uses.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

Configures standard revocation checks for TLS certificates.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Only End Entities</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Which elements to verify in the certificate chain:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Verify only the last element in the certificate chain.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Verify all elements in the certificate chain.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prefer Crls</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>How to check certificate validity:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Check the Certification Revocation List (CRL) for certificate validity.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Use the Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP) to check certificate validity.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>No Fallback</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use the secondary method to check certificate validity:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Use the method that wasnât specified in the <strong>Prefer Crls</strong> field (the secondary method) to check certificate validity.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Do not use the secondary method to check certificate validity.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Soft Fail</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>What to do if the revocation server canât be reached or is busy:</p><p>* <code>true</code></p><p>Avoid verification failure.</p><p>* <code>false</code></p><p>Allow the verification to fail.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder

Configures a custom OCSP responder for certification revocation checks.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the OCSP responder.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Cert Alias</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Alias of the signing certificate for the OCSP response. If specified, the alias must be in the truststore.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### CRL File

Specifies the location of the certification revocation list (CRL) file.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path to the CRL file.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnection

Configures a reconnection strategy for an operation.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Fails Deployment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to <code>true</code>, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#reconnect-forever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Reconnection strategy to use.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How many reconnection attempts to make.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Reconnect Forever

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Frequency</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>How often in milliseconds to reconnect.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Expiration Policy

Configures an expiration policy strategy.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Idle Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the maximum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Max Idle Time</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Subscribe Channel Attributes

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Channel</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable In Memory Stream

Configures the in-memory streaming strategy by which the request fails if the data exceeds the MAX
buffer size. Always run performance tests to find the optimal buffer size for your specific use
case.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Initial Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Initial amount of memory to allocate to the data stream. If the streamed data exceeds this value, the buffer expands by <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, with an upper limit of <strong>Max In Memory Size value</strong>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Size Increment</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>This is by how much the buffer size expands if it exceeds its initial size. Setting a value of zero or lower means that the buffer should not expand, meaning that a STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED error is raised when the buffer gets full.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum size of the buffer. If the buffer size exceeds this value, Mule raises a <code>STREAM_MAXIMUM_SIZE_EXCEEDED</code> error. A value of less than or equal to <code>0</code> means no limit.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Unit for the <strong>Initial Buffer Size</strong>, <strong>Buffer Size Increment</strong>, and <strong>Buffer Unit</strong> fields.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Repeatable File Store Stream

Configures the repeatable file-store streaming strategy by which Mule keeps a portion of the stream
content in memory. If the stream content is larger than the configured buffer size, Mule backs up
the bufferâs content to disk and then clears the memory.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>In Memory Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum amount of memory that the stream can use for data. If the amount of memory exceeds this value, Mule buffers the content to disk. To optimize performance:</p><p>* Configure a larger buffer size to avoid the number of times Mule needs to write the buffer on disk. This increases performance, but it also limits the number of concurrent requests your application can process, because it requires additional memory.</p><p>* Configure a smaller buffer size to decrease memory load at the expense of response time.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Buffer Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>BYTE</p></li><li><p>KB</p></li><li><p>MB</p></li><li><p>GB</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Unit for the <strong>In Memory Size</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Non-repeatable Stream

Disables the repeatable stream functionality and use non-repeatable streams to have less performance
overhead, memory use, and cost.

### Redelivery Policy

Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a
redelivery policy to any source in a flow.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Max Redelivery Count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of times that a redelivered request can be processed unsuccessfully before returning a REDELIVERY_EXHAUSTED error.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Secure Hash</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If <code>true</code>, Mule uses a secure hash algorithm to identify a redelivered message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Digest Algorithm</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Secure hashing algorithm to use if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>true</code>. If the payload of the message is a Java object, Mule ignores this value and returns the value that the payloadâs <code>hashCode()</code> returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Id Expression</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>One or more expressions that determine when a message was redelivered. You can set this property only if the <strong>Use Secure Hash</strong> field is <code>false</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object Store</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores the redelivery counter for each message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Redis Connector 5.6

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

Anypoint Studio (Studio) editors help you design and update your Mule applications, properties, and
configuration files.

To add and configure a connector in Studio:

1.  [Create a Mule project](#create-mule-project).
2.  [Add the connector to your Mule project](#add-connector-to-project).
3.  [Configure an input source for the connectorâs flow](#configure-input-source).
4.  [Add a connector operation to the flow](#add-connector-operation).
5.  [Configure a global element for the connector](#configure-global-element).

When you run your app, you can view the app log as described in [View the App Log](#view-app-log).

If you are new to configuring connectors in Studio, see
[Using Anypoint Studio to Configure a Connector](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-use-studio).
If, after reading this topic, you need additional information about the connector fields, see the
[Redis Connector 5.3 Reference](redis-connector-reference).

## Create a Mule Project

In Studio, create a new Mule project in which to add and configure the connector:

1.  In Studio, select **File > New > Mule Project**.
2.  Enter a name for your Mule project and click **Finish**.

## Add the Connector to Your Mule Project

Add Aynpoint Connector for Redis to your Mule project to automatically populate the XML code with
the connectorâs namespace and schema location and add the required dependencies to the projectâs
`pom.xml` file:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, click **(X) Search in Exchange**.
2.  In **Add Dependencies to Project**, type `redis` in the search field.
3.  Click **Redis Connector** in **Available modules**.
4.  Click **Add**.
5.  Click **Finish**.

Adding a connector to a Mule project in Studio does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Studio workspace.

## Configure an Input Source

An input source initiates a flow when a specified condition is met. You can configure one of these
input sources to use with Redis Connector:

- **Subscribe**  
  Enables the Mule app to listen to specified channels
- **HTTP Listener**  
  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port
- **Scheduler**  
  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure the **Subscribe** input source, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Redis > Subscribe**.
2.  Drag **Subscribe** to the Studio canvas.
3.  On the **Subscribe** configuration screen, optionally change the value of the **Display Name**
    field.
4.  In **Channels** provide a list of Redis channel names or globbing patterns.

## Add a Connector Operation to the Flow

When you add a connector operation to your flow, you immediately define a specific operation for
that connector to perform.

To add an operation for Redis Connector, follow these steps:

1.  In the **Mule Palette** view, select **Redis** and then select the desired operation.
2.  Drag the operation onto the Studio canvas to the right of the input source.

## Configure a Global Element for the Connector

When you configure a connector, itâs best to configure a global element that all instances of that
connector in the app can use. Configuring a global element requires you to provide the
authentication credentials the connector uses to access the target Redis system. Redis Connector
supports global elements for clustered Redis configurations and non-clustered Redis configurations.

1.  Select the operation in the Studio canvas.
2.  In the configuration screen for the operation, click the plus sign (+) next to the **Connector
    configuration** field to configure a global element that can be used by all instances of Redis
    Connector in the app.

    When you configure a global element, you can reference a configuration file that contains
    ANT-style property placeholders (recommended), or you can enter your authorization credentials
    in the global configuration properties. For information about the benefits of using property
    placeholders and how to configure them, see
    [Anypoint Connector Configuration](../../connectors/introduction/intro-connector-configuration-overview).

3.  On the **Connection** tab, select the connection type:
    - **[Clustered](#clustered-config)**
    - **[NonClustered](#nonclustered-config)**
    - **[Sentinel](#sentinel-config)**

4.  Click **Test Connection** to confirm that Mule can connect with the specified server.
5.  Click **OK**.

### Clustered Redis Configuration

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure a Clustered connection:

![Configuration fields for Redis clustered connection type](_images/redis-basic-config-clustered.png)

1.  In **Name**, enter a name for the configuration.
2.  In **Connection**, select **Clustered** to use the connector with a clustered Redis
    configuration.
3.  Enter values for the following fields:
    - **Cluster hosts**  
      Comma-separated list for all Redis cluster hosts using the format `host:port`
    - **Connection timeout**  
      Optional connection timeout value, in milliseconds
    - **Entry Ttl** Optional default time-to-live value to set on keys stored through the connector
      **Password**  
      Redis password for server authentication

4.  Click **Test Connection**.
5.  Click **OK**.

### Nonclustered Redis Configuration

Enter the following information on the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties** screen
to configure a non-clustered connection:

![Configuration fields for Redis non-clustered connection type](_images/redis-basic-config.png)

1.  In **Name**, enter a name for the configuration.
2.  In **Connection**, select **NonClustered** to use the connector with a non-clustered Redis
    configuration.
3.  Enter values for the following fields:
    - **Host**  
      Host for the Redis server
    - **Port**  
      Port on which the non-clustered server is running **Connection Timeout**  
      Optional connection timeout value, in milliseconds
    - **Entry Ttl**  
      Optional default time-to-live value to set on keys stored through the connector.
    - **Password**  
      Password for server authentication.

4.  Click **Test Connection**.
5.  Click **OK**.

## Sentinel Configuration

Sentinel provides a high-availability solution in case of failure on your Redis cluster.

To configure a Sentinel connection:

1.  On the **General** tab of the **Global Element Properties**, enter a name for the configuration.
2.  In **Connection**, select **Sentinel** to use the connector with a non-clustered Redis
    configuration.
3.  Enter values for the following fields:
    - **Sentinels hosts**  
      Sentinel addresses that monitor the Redis master host to which the connector will connect
    - **Master name**  
      Name of the Redis master for which Sentinel discovers and returns the address of the master
      instance to connect to
    - **Sentinel password**  
      Password for Sentinel server authentication
    - **Index**  
      Index of the database that the pool uses
    - **Password**  
      Password for Redis server authentication
    - **Entry ttl**  
      Optional default time-to-live value to set on keys stored through the connector

4.  Click **Test Connection**.
5.  Click **OK**.

### TLS Configuration

To enable and configure TLS for your app:

1.  In the **Connection** section for the configuration, click the **Security** tab.
2.  To configure a truststore, in **TLS Configuration**, select **Edit inline** and provide the
    following values:
    - **Path**  
      Location of the truststore file.
    - **Password**  
      Password for the truststore file.
    - **Type**  
      File format of the truststore file.
    - **Algorithm**  
      Algorithm the truststore uses.
    - **Insecure**  
      Boolean that determines whether or not to validate the truststore. If set to `true`, no
      validation occurs. The default value is `false`.

3.  To configure a keystore, enter the following values:
    - **Type**  
      Optionally specify the file format of the keystore file. The default value is `JKS`.
    - **Path**  
      Optionally specify the location of the keystore file. This can be used for two-way
      authentication for the connector.
    - **Alias**  
      Attribute that indicates the alias of the key to use when the keystore contains many private
      keys. If not defined, the first key in the file is used by default.
    - **Key password**  
      Key manager password, which is the password for the private key inside the keystore.
    - **Password**  
      Store password for the keystore file. This is required only if the **Key Store Location** is
      configured.
      - **Algorithm**  
        Algorithm used in the keystore.

4.  Click **Test Connection**.
5.  Click **OK**.

## View the App Log

You can view the app log for the connector as follows:

- If youâre running the app from the Anypoint Platform, the output is visible in the Anypoint Studio
  **Console** window.
- If youâre running the app using Mule from the command line, the app log is visible in your OS
  console.

Unless the log file path was customized in the appâs log file (`log4j2.xml`), you can also view the
app log in `MULE_HOME/logs/<app-name>.log`.

For more information about the app log, see
[Configuring Logging](../../mule-runtime/latest/logging-in-mule).

## Next Step

After you configure a global element and connection information, configure the other fields for the
connector.

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Redis Connector 5.6 XML and Maven Support

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If you manually code a Mule runtime engine (Mule) app in XML, either from the Anypoint Studio XML
editor or from a text editor, you can get access to the connector in your app by adding reference
statements to both your XML Mule flow and the Apache Maven `pom.xml` file.

When you install the connector in Studio, Studio automatically populates the XML code with the
connectorâs namespace and schema location. It also adds a dependency to the `pom.xml` file.

## Add a Namespace for the Connector

Paste the following code inside the `<mule>` tag of the header of your configuration XML:

```xml
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis/current/mule-redis.xsd">
```

This example shows how the namespace statements are placed in the `<mule>` XML block:

```xml
<mule xmlns:redis="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis"
	xmlns:tracking="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking"
	xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http"
	xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core"
	xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
	xmlns:spring="http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans"
	xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
	xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans
	http://www.springframework.org/schema/beans/spring-beans-current.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/tracking/current/mule-tracking-ee.xsd
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis
	http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/redis/current/mule-redis.xsd">
```

## Add a POM File Dependency

The Apache Maven `pom.xml` file generated by Anypoint Studio contains dependencies for a Mule app.
If you code a Mule app manually, include this XML snippet in your `pom.xml` file to enable access to
this connector:

```xml
<dependency>
  <groupId>com.mulesoft.connectors</groupId>
  <artifactId>mule-redis-connector</artifactId>
  <version>x.x.x</version>
  <classifier>mule-plugin</classifier>
</dependency>
```

Replace `x.x.x` with the version that corresponds to the connector you are using.

To obtain the most up-to-date `pom.xml` file information:

1.  Go to [Anypoint Exchange](https://www.mulesoft.com/exchange/).
2.  In Exchange, click **Login** and supply your Anypoint Platform username and password.
3.  In Exchange, search for `redis`.
4.  Select the connector.
5.  Click **Dependency Snippets** near the upper right of the screen.

## Next

After completing your namespace and `pom.xml` file, you can try the Redis
[Examples](redis-connector-examples).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
